steps.title: Find Blake's role
steps.content: The text introduces Ellison, then focuses on Harold Blake's afterword to Little, Big. Blake praises the novel because it blends Schiller's naive and sentimental modes, so the passage mainly explains why a critic admires a particular novel.

steps.title: Rule out broader purposes
steps.content: Choice A is wrong because the text says Little, Big resists a strict two-category split. Choice B is wrong because Owen Mercer is only mentioned in passing. Choice C is wrong because no inspiration for Ellison's writing is discussed. The answer is D.
